Title: User logs in successfully
Preconditions: User has registered and stored credentials in the database.
Test Steps:
1. User enters username and password.
2. User clicks on the login button.
3. System sends request to the database.
4. Database verifies the credentials.
5. Database returns success response.
6. System redirects to the main page.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the main page with a success message.

Title: User logs in with incorrect password
Preconditions: User has registered and stored credentials in the database.
Test Steps:
1. User enters username and password.
2. User clicks on the login button.
3. System sends request to the database.
4. Database verifies the credentials.
5. Database returns failure response.
6. System displays an error message.
Expected Result: User sees an error message indicating incorrect password.

Preconditions: User has registered and stored credentials in the database.

Title: User registers successfully
Test Data: Username: testuser, Password: testpassword, Email: test@example.com
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User fills in the registration form with the provided data.
3. User submits the form.
4. System sends request to the database.
5. Database stores the user data.
6. Database returns success response.
7. System redirects to the login page.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the login page with a success message.

Title: User attempts to register with existing username
Preconditions: User with the same username already exists.
Test Data: Username: testuser, Password: testpassword, Email: test@example.com
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User fills in the registration form with the provided data.
3. User submits the form.
4. System sends request to the database.
5. Database checks for existing username.
6. Database returns failure response.
7. System displays an error message.
Expected Result: User sees an error message indicating the username is already taken.

Title: User updates personal information successfully
Preconditions: User is logged in and has personal information stored in the database.
Test Data: New email: newemail@example.com, New phone number: 1234567890
Test Steps:

2. User fills in the new information.
3. User clicks on the save button.
4. System sends request to the database.
5. Database updates the user information.
6. Database returns success response.
7. System redirects to the personal info page.
Expected Result: User sees the updated information on the personal info page.

Title: User fails to update personal information due to invalid email format
